what_it_does: This file defines a small runtime mechanism that embeds a fixed, hand-authored characterization of AI-generated prose deformation and persists the resulting vector as a planted anchor (anchor://ai-corner) in a vector-store space, using the same local embedder bridge that built the corpus so the resulting cosines remain comparable.
observations: Defines a dense, keyword-avoiding prose constant AI_CORNER_TEXT describing AI deformation modes.
Uses urllib to POST to a local bridge endpoint /api/cognition/embed and expects {output: {vector, dim, model}}.
Raises RuntimeError if the embedder returns no vector (fail-loud).
Computes a SHA-256 content hash of the anchor text and passes it to the store.
Calls store.space_address and store.put_vector, implying an abstract store interface not shown here.
Provides a convenience function plant_ai_corner that plants the pole in the default space 'topics'.
Has a __main__ block that imports FsStore and fabric config from the project root and runs plant_ai_corner.
Docstrings repeatedly stress same-lens embedding and honest, non-oracle use of the planted pole.
standouts: The AI-pole is supplied by the system's own explicit characterization of AI deformation rather than a corpus document.
Strong emphasis on embedding through the same lens as items to keep cosines meaningful.
Idempotency by content hash rather than by source alone.
conventions: Type information lives in docstrings rather than inline annotations.
Default bridge URL is a hardcoded localhost string.
__main__ block mutates sys.path to find local modules.
concerns: Hard dependency on a live localhost embedder bridge at a fixed port.
No retry, circuit breaker, or graceful degradation for the network call.
Store interface is assumed but not validated or documented in this file.
Default model fallback string 'BAAI/bge-m3' is used if the response omits model metadata.
Main block will fail if fabric.config or store.fs_store are absent or have different names.
notes: A narrow, self-aware utility that plants a synthetic semantic pole so a downstream separator can treat AI-deformation as a named gravity origin.
questions: What is the exact contract of store.space_address and store.put_vector?
How is the content hash used to enforce idempotency inside the store?
What produces the separation_report mentioned in the module docstring?
Is the AI_CORNER_TEXT versioned or tracked when it changes?
What happens if the bridge is live but its embed role is not BGE-M3?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose and implementation match: it plants the AI-corner anchor vector through the same embedder and persists it in a store space.
intention_signals: The file is trying to give a two-gravity separator a named, comparable AI-origin pole without pretending the pole itself is a verified corpus finding.
novelty: It explicitly encodes the AI's own model of AI-prose deformation as a planted vector anchor, making the pollution direction an inspectable, embeddable artifact rather than an implicit assumption.
contribution: Provides a concrete mechanism to embed and persist a planted semantic pole (anchor://ai-corner) in the same embedding space as corpus items, so separator bindings can reference an AI-deformation direction that is content-addressed and embedded through the same model.
summary_for_embedding: A runtime module that embeds a hand-authored characterization of AI-prose deformation through a local embedder bridge and persists the resulting vector as a planted anchor in a vector-store space.
intention_for_embedding: To create a comparable, same-lens AI-corner pole that a two-gravity separator can bind as the AI-gravity origin without treating it as a verified corpus item.

symbols.does: POSTs text to the local embedder bridge and returns vector metadata.
symbols.description: Sends a JSON request to /api/cognition/embed, parses the returned vector, dimension, and model, and raises RuntimeError if no vector is returned, ensuring the anchor is embedded by the same model as the corpus items.

symbols.does: Embeds the given text and persists the vector under the source identifier in a store space.
symbols.description: Computes a SHA-256 content hash of the text, resolves a space address for the source, and calls store.put_vector with the vector and metadata; re-planting the same text is intended to overwrite the same entry.

symbols.does: Plants the anchor://ai-corner pole in the specified embedding space.
symbols.description: Convenience wrapper around plant_anchor that uses the module's AI_CORNER_SOURCE and AI_CORNER_TEXT constants, defaulting to the 'topics' space.
